calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Aug-01-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Mayo for Marie Chan.

The Court stated that it appears notice was given to the potential parties who will take subject to the disclaimers if they are appropriate, and that if the disclaimers are not appropriate, they will need to proceed with probate to achieve what the parties want to do. Ms. Mayo stated that she is willing to file probate, and that it is her understanding that the parties want all of the property to remain in the family/the status quo which is passing it on from generation to generation. The Court stated that it is inclined to find that the disclaimers are effective and that the property will have to go through probate. The Petition for Spousal Property is denied without prejudice to how ever this plays out in the probate proceeding. Ms. Mayo is to prepare an order. The Court Clerk informed Ms. Mayo that she needs to use the same case number when filing the probate petition and that she is to speak with the Probate Examiner to confirm.

Judge: Peter J. Buscht. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jul-18-2018 Continued To Aug-01-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jun-06-2018 Continued To Jul-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Mayo for Marie Chan.

Ms. Mayo stated that her Client is in agreement with the Probate Examiner's opinion that the spousal petition can only confirm 50% of the decedent's separate property to the spouse and the other 50% to the adult children. The Court stated that the problem is disclaimers were filed, and that if the disclaimers are effective, they are irrevocable. Ms. Mayo stated that there is no ruling as to whether the disclaimers are time barred, that she filed declarations from each of the adult children yesterday and that she thinks there are other potential heirs. The Petition for Spousal Property is continued to July 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for notice to be given to the prospective heirs.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
